chunk_summary: This section explores the role of anemia and iron deficiency in chronic growth faltering, emphasizing interventions like Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS) and National Deworming Day (NDD) guidelines to combat stunting and undernutrition in India.
learning_objectives: Understand the impact of anemia and iron deficiency on chronic growth faltering and stunting.
Learn about the Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS) program and its implementation.
Examine the role of deworming in addressing chronic undernutrition and anemia.
Review dietary guidelines for preventing iron deficiency and promoting growth.
key_facts: Anemia and iron deficiency are significant contributors to stunting and chronic growth faltering in children.
The Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS) program targets adolescents and women of reproductive age to prevent anemia.
National Deworming Day (NDD) guidelines recommend biannual deworming for children aged 1-19 years to reduce parasitic infections.
The 2024 Dietary Guidelines for Indians emphasize iron-rich diets and supplementation to combat nutritional deficiencies.
Iron deficiency affects cognitive development and physical growth, particularly in children under five years of age.
reference_quote: The Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS) program aims to reduce anemia prevalence among adolescents and women of reproductive age through weekly supplementation and education.
document_key_topics: Anemia and its role in stunting
Iron and folic acid supplementation programs
